content:
Hello folks, I am currently testing Payload ecommerce and Im having thoughts... biggest concern is its in beta and looks like there is no active community around (specifically in ecommerce - though I might be wrong in this one)
For background, below are the reasons why I am leaning on Payload right now:

• client doesnt want to use and pay for Shopify subscription

• freedom on front end and loads fast - thus we decided to go for headless route

• should be able to handle more than 200 products. each product has 2 variants categories and more than 10 to choose from (product is curtains - there are many sizes available and customer can choose fabric type, therefore variants are massive)

• client's vps is 2GB ram with already have some low traffic static Astro sites on it (client is willing to consider upgrading or having the ecommerce site in separate vps if i prove that its really required)
When I researched using AI, given the current VPS ram, the suggested site built is Payload + Next.js but its admin dashboard seems not so intuitive for the client and I have doubts on its reliability
Im thinking whether to test Medusa or headless Woocommerce. If admin dashboard will be more intuitive than payload plus if its not in beta and has an active community, I can convince the client to upgrade the VPS or have it on a separate VPS so the VPS limitation mentioned above is not really a hard requirement...
If you're in my shoes, what would you use?

  submitted by   /u/aomorimemory
[link]   [comments]
